title: Predetermined Overhead Rate & Application
event: Reyes Co. estimates annual factory overhead of $200,000 and 25,000 direct labor hours. Job 314 used 340 direct labor hours. Compute the predetermined overhead rate and the overhead applied to Job 314.

computationPaths.narration: The rate is set BEFORE the year from estimates, so jobs can be costed in real time instead of waiting for December's actual bills.

memorize.body: POHR = ESTIMATED overhead ÷ ESTIMATED activity base. Applied = POHR × ACTUAL base used by the job.
Estimates set the rate; actuals drive the application. Mixing that up is the chapter's signature error.

questions.prompt: POHR $8/DLH, Job 314 used 340 DLH. Overhead applied = ?

distractors.feedback: Waiting for actual overhead defeats the system's purpose — jobs are costed NOW using the predetermined rate.
That's the hours. Multiply by the $8 rate to convert activity into applied cost.
